name: prog
description: Programmer tooling — the developer-experience surface for actors with the programmer flag. The same world is the IDE: prog tools introspect and mutate the running object graph, with no design-time/runtime split. The surface is intentionally small (~10 verbs) so an agent can hold the whole authoring loop in head: inspect → resolve_verb → search to read; create → set_property → install_verb to write; compile and trace to validate. This is LambdaCore's builder/programmer/editor surface, MCP-shaped: actor-scoped target resolution, explicit define-vs-program modes, and inspection that makes inheritance and impact visible. All verbs are MCP-exposed (tool_exposed: true) and progbit-gated (E_PERM when actor lacks flags.programmer or flags.wizard). Each verb is a thin wrapper around a prog_* engine builtin where permission, version-conflict, and CRUD semantics actually live; the verbs handle progbit gating and observation emission.

classes.description: Singleton holder for programmer tooling. `the_prog` intentionally merges LambdaCore's builder and programmer command surfaces into one MCP-reachable object; this is only a UI convenience, not a new authority model. The seed hook creates `the_prog` at install time. Programmers reach the tools by adding `the_prog` to their MCP focus set (woo_focus the_prog) — the catalog does not automatically grant reachability so non-programmer actors don't see the tools by accident. A future bootstrap could focus `the_prog` automatically on grant of the programmer flag; until then the focus step is explicit.

verbs.name: look
inspect
resolve_verb
search
create
chparent
recycle
set_property
install_verb
compile
trace
_assert_progbit

seed_hooks.name: Programmer Tools
seed_hooks.description: Programmer-tooling singleton. Add to your MCP focus set with woo_focus(the_prog) when authoring; remove with woo_unfocus(the_prog) when not. Verbs are gated on the programmer flag — wizards have full access, programmers can act on their own objects, others get E_PERM.
